title: Aaliya and the Magic Paint Set
synopsis: When Aaliya finds a magic paint set in her grandmother's attic, her drawings come to life! She must go on a colorful adventure to help a painted butterfly find its way home before the magic fades.

pages.text: Aaliya loved visiting Grandma Noor's house. It was full of treasures and secrets. Today, she was exploring the dusty attic. "What's in this old trunk?" Aaliya wondered. She opened the creaky lid.

illustration.description: A curious young girl with dark curly hair and bright brown eyes kneeling beside an ornate wooden trunk in a cozy attic. Sunlight streams through a small window, illuminating dust motes. The scene has warm golden tones.
illustration.alt: Aaliya opening a treasure trunk in the attic

pages.text: Inside the trunk was a beautiful paint set. The paints sparkled like jewels. Red like rubies. Blue like sapphires. Yellow like sunshine. "These are the prettiest paints ever!" Aaliya said. She picked up a small paintbrush.

illustration.description: Close-up of magical paint tubes and brushes glowing with inner light in rich jewel tones. Aaliya's excited face peers over the paint set, her eyes wide with wonder. The paints shimmer with magical sparkles.
illustration.alt: Aaliya discovering the magical paint set

pages.text: Aaliya found some paper and started to paint. She painted a beautiful orange butterfly. Suddenly, the butterfly wiggled its wings! It fluttered right off the paper! "Oh my!" gasped Aaliya. "You're alive!"

illustration.description: A magical orange butterfly with delicate wings lifting off a piece of paper while Aaliya watches in amazement. Her mouth is open in surprise and delight. Magical sparkles surround the butterfly as it comes to life.
illustration.alt: The painted butterfly coming to life

pages.text: The little butterfly looked sad. "Hello," it said in a tiny voice. "I'm lost. I don't know where painted butterflies live." Aaliya felt sorry for her new friend. "Don't worry," she said. "I'll help you find your home!"

illustration.description: Aaliya sitting cross-legged on the attic floor talking to a small orange butterfly perched on her finger. The butterfly has a sad expression. Aaliya looks kind and determined to help.
illustration.alt: Aaliya talking to the sad butterfly

pages.text: "Maybe you need a garden," Aaliya said. She dipped her brush in green paint. She painted grass and flowers. The grass grew tall and tickled her feet! Pink flowers bloomed with sweet smells.

illustration.description: Magical painted grass and flowers springing up around Aaliya and the butterfly in the attic. The plants are vibrant and alive, with pink and purple flowers blooming. Aaliya laughs as grass tickles her bare feet.
illustration.alt: Magical garden growing in the attic

pages.text: The butterfly flew around the flowers. But it still looked sad. "This is pretty," it sighed. "But it doesn't feel like home." Aaliya thought hard. What did butterflies need? "I know!" she said. "You need other butterflies!"

illustration.description: The orange butterfly sitting sadly on a painted flower while Aaliya has a lightbulb moment, her finger pointed up with excitement. The magical garden surrounds them with blooming flowers in the background.
illustration.alt: Aaliya having an idea to help the butterfly

pages.text: Aaliya painted more butterflies. Yellow ones and purple ones. Blue ones with spots. Soon the attic was full of fluttering friends! "Welcome home," said Aaliya. Her orange butterfly smiled big.

illustration.description: The attic transformed into a magical butterfly garden with colorful butterflies of all sizes flying around. Aaliya stands in the center with arms outstretched, smiling joyfully as butterflies dance around her and the flowers.
illustration.alt: The attic full of painted butterflies

pages.text: "Thank you, Aaliya!" said the orange butterfly. "Now I have a family." All the butterflies did a happy dance in the air. Just then, Grandma Noor came upstairs. "I see you found my old paint set," she smiled.

illustration.description: Grandma Noor, an elderly woman with silver hair and kind eyes wearing a colorful headscarf, standing in the doorway smiling warmly. Butterflies flutter around her and Aaliya in the magical garden setting.

pages.text: "It was magic, Grandma!" Aaliya said. Grandma Noor winked. "The best kind of magic comes from helping others," she said. Aaliya hugged her grandma tight. The butterflies danced around them both. What a perfect day!

illustration.description: Aaliya and Grandma Noor embracing in the magical butterfly garden, surrounded by colorful butterflies and blooming flowers. Both have warm, loving expressions. Magical sparkles and soft golden light fill the scene.
illustration.alt: Aaliya and Grandma hugging in the magical garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Where did Aaliya find the magic paint set?
questions.explanation: Aaliya found the magic paint set in her Grandma Noor's attic, inside an old trunk.

questions.question: What was the first thing Aaliya painted that came to life?
questions.explanation: Aaliya painted a beautiful orange butterfly, which came to life and fluttered off the paper.

questions.question: Why was the butterfly sad?
choices: It was lost and didn't know where painted butterflies live
questions.explanation: The butterfly was sad because it was lost and didn't know where painted butterflies were supposed to live.

questions.question: How did Aaliya help the butterfly feel at home?
choices: She painted a garden and more butterflies
questions.explanation: Aaliya painted a garden with flowers and then painted more butterflies so her first butterfly would have a family and feel at home.

questions.question: What did Grandma Noor say about the best kind of magic?
questions.explanation: Grandma Noor told Aaliya that the best kind of magic comes from helping others.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from the story!
pages.text: Aaliya found a magic _____ set in her grandma's attic. She painted an orange _____ that came to life! The butterfly was _____ because it didn't know where to live. Aaliya painted a garden and more butterflies to help her new _____ feel at home.

pages.title: What Happened Next?
pages.instructions: Put the story events in order!
pages.text: First, Aaliya _____ the magic paint set. Then, she painted a butterfly that came to _____. The butterfly felt sad and _____. Finally, Aaliya painted more butterflies so it would have a _____.
